title: Order Processing & Payment

steps: START: Validate session (userName, password) — if null → redirect to login.jsp
→ Create TransactionBean(userName, paidAmount) — generates transactionId + timestamp
[LOOP] for each CartBean item in cartItems:
→ addOrder(order) → DB: INSERT INTO orders(transid, prodid, qty, amount, shipped=0)
→ CartServiceImpl.removeAProduct(userId, prodId) → DB: delete cart item
→ addTransaction(transaction) → DB: INSERT INTO transactions(transid, username, time, amount)
→ MailMessage.transactionSuccess(userName, firstName, transId, amount) → send email
OrderServlet forwards to orderDetails.jsp, injects status message via JS
START: Validate session (usertype) — if null → redirect to login.jsp with 'Access Denied'
Parse orderId, prodId, userName, amount from request params
→ DB: UPDATE orders SET shipped=1 WHERE orderid=? AND prodid=? AND shipped=0
[DECISION] rows updated > 0 → status = 'Order Has been shipped successfully!!'
→ MailMessage.orderShipped(userName, firstName, orderId, amount) → send email
Forward to pagename JSP, inject status message via JS
INSERT INTO orders VALUES(transactionId, prodId, quantity, amount, 0)
[DECISION] k > 0 → return true | SQLException → return false
INSERT INTO transactions VALUES(transId, userName, transDateTime, transAmount)
[DECISION] k > 0 → return true | SQLException → return false
SELECT username FROM transactions WHERE transid=?
Return userId string (used externally for lookup)

key_decisions: Session valid? → proceed or redirect to login
Cart is empty? → abort with failure status
Each cart item: addOrder success? → continue or break
Each cart item: removeAProduct success? → continue or break
Each cart item: sellNProduct success? → continue or break
All items processed (ordered==true)? → addTransaction or fail
addTransaction success? → send email + success status or fail
shipNow rows updated > 0? → success + send email or FAILURE
key_relationships: OrderServlet depends_on OrderServiceImpl (paymentSuccess, shipNow — via ShipmentServlet)
data_objects: OrderBean: transactionId, productId, quantity, amount, shipped (0=pending, 1=shipped)
OrderDetails: orderId, prodImage, prodName, qty, amount, time, productId, shipped
important_notes: paymentSuccess is transactional in intent but NOT wrapped in a DB transaction — partial failures can leave orphaned orders
shipped column uses 0/1 int flag; shipNow only updates if shipped=0 (idempotent guard)
TransactionBean auto-generates transactionId (likely via IDUtil) at construction time
MailMessage sends email on both payment success and shipment — relies on JavaMailUtil
TransServiceImpl.getUserId is a lightweight lookup used externally (e.g., admin views)
